title: Peppery Argentinian Coconut Stir-fry
description: A romantic and tender dish featuring peppery flavors with coconut cream in a whole30-friendly twist, inspired by traditional Argentine cuisine.
instructions: Marinate the chicken in a mixture of coconut cream, lemon juice, salt, and freshly ground pepper for at least an hour.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook until golden brown on both sides (about 5 minutes per side).
Remove chicken from pan and set aside. In the same pan, add onions and garlic to sweat until translucent (about 2-3 minutes), then add peppers.
Sauté for another 2-3 minutes until slightly softened. Add coconut cream and let it simmer until it thickens a bit (about 5 minutes).
Return chicken to the pan with its juices, stir-fry for an additional minute, allowing flavors to meld together.
Serve hot as part of your dinner or alongside a whole grain side dish.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
